concise_steps: Add caching for Dart/Flutter `pub` cache and Gradle cache in CI (cache ~/.pub-cache, ~/.gradle)
Cache Android build outputs and emulator images between runs
Cache node/npm/yarn if any web tooling is used
Parallelize build/test jobs where safe and use job-level cache keys
Enable `org.gradle.caching=true` and configure `org.gradle.parallel=true`
Set JVM heap options for Gradle daemon in `gradle.properties`
Use Gradle configuration on demand and avoid expensive init scripts in CI
Use `--split-per-abi` or build Android App Bundle (AAB) to reduce per-device download size
Strip debug symbols and remove unnecessary native libs for release
Enable code shrinking (R8) and review keep rules to avoid over-keeping
Convert PNG/JPEG assets to WebP or AVIF where supported (lossy for photos, lossless/WebP for UI)
Run bulk compression (libvips/mozjpeg/oxipng) and commit optimized assets
Automate image optimization step in CI for new/changed assets
Identify unused assets referenced in `pubspec.yaml` and remove them
Static-analyze project to find dead Dart code and remove or lazy-load it
Add a CI lint step that fails on unreferenced assets to prevent regressions
Defer building heavy widgets until visible (use `Visibility`, `IndexedStack`, or route-based lazy init)
Use `cached_network_image` or equivalent with placeholder + fade-in for remote images
Avoid preloading all images; preload only above-the-fold images

files_affected: server-side code that handles EPG (if present)
concise_steps: Ensure EPG data is cached with appropriate TTLs and incremental updates instead of full refreshes
Index EPG data by channel + time range to avoid full-scan queries
Use compressed binary/JSON formats (ndjson/gzip) for bulk EPG transfers and enable client delta-sync

suggestions.area: Backend connection pooling & HTTP client reuse
concise_steps: Reuse HTTP client/session objects instead of new connection per request
Introduce DB connection pools (psycopg2 pool, SQLAlchemy pool) where applicable
Use keep-alive and tune pool sizes to expected concurrency
Move heavy CPU-bound work (transcription, encoding) to isolates or separate worker processes
Use async IO for network tasks and thread pools for blocking I/O
Batch work and use queues (e.g., RabbitMQ, Pub/Sub, background job runner) to avoid UI stalls
Serve large static files (EPG, manifests, images) from a CDN with long cache TTL and cache-busting hashes
Set correct `Cache-Control` and `ETag`/`Last-Modified` headers
Use signed URLs for private large artifacts and allow CDN caching for public parts

files_affected: any Firestore usage in server or client code (lib/**)
concise_steps: Add composite indexes for common compound queries to avoid collection scans
Audit queries to avoid client-side filtering of large result sets
Use paginated queries and limit projections to required fields
Defer non-essential init (analytics, heavy network calls) until after first render
Load critical config from disk first then lazy-load optional config
Initialize player components only when playback requested
